label: Autoencoders

node.label: Autoencoders
node.definition: An autoencoder compresses an input into a latent code and reconstructs it, learning representations through an information bottleneck rather than explicit labels.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Autoencoders is to connect Affine transforms & dense layers, Reconstruction objectives to downstream capabilities including Variational autoencoders, VQ-VAE & learned discrete tokenizers, Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis.
examples: substrate for VQ-VAE & learned discrete tokenizers
substrate for Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Affine transforms & dense layers, Reconstruction objectives.

dependencies.rationale: Autoencoders is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
Autoencoders is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Reconstruction objectives.
Autoencoders consumes, emits, or organizes information using Latent variables & hidden state.

sentence_units.text: An autoencoder compresses an input into a latent code and reconstructs it, learning representations through an information bottleneck rather than explicit labels.

The role of Autoencoders is to connect Affine transforms & dense layers, Reconstruction objectives to downstream capabilities including Variational autoencoders, VQ-VAE & learned discrete tokenizers, Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis.
